codeQualityAssessment.architecture: Clean implementation with well-separated concerns. STE quantization is properly implemented using PyTorch autograd.Function.
codeQualityAssessment.readability: Good documentation with comprehensive docstrings. Code follows PEP 8 style (after lint fixes).
codeQualityAssessment.testability: Excellent test coverage with 39 test cases covering all major functionality including edge cases.
codeQualityAssessment.maintainability: Good use of type hints and clear structure. State management is well-designed.

VAL-QNT-001.evidence: test_4bit_round_trip_error passes - 4-bit quantization achieves <5% relative error

VAL-QNT-002.evidence: test_1bit_values_are_binary passes - values are exactly -1 or +1

VAL-QNT-004.evidence: test_4bit_gradient_flow, test_1bit_gradient_flow, test_tiered_quantization_gradient_flow all verify STE works correctly

sharedStateObservations.description: PyTorch autograd.Function STE pattern: For STE quantization, implement forward() to apply quantization and backward() to pass gradients straight through. This is the canonical approach for differentiable quantization.
Symmetric quantization convention: Scale computed as max_abs / 7 for 4-bit (using range [-7, 7] for stability). This avoids -8 which can cause round-trip issues.
